reviews.review_msg: George makes theory meaningful. Course should be spread over 2 semesters. Great guy, knows exactly how deep the reading list is, but wants to give you a real milestone achievement - don't miss the final lecture for amazing cake and the Anointing of the Trowels with Real Dirt from Famous Sites!

This class is very text-heavy, and I wouldn't advise it with a heavy course load if you actually want to read everything. He gives reading guides so you aren't totally lost when reading. His feedback is actually very helpful, and he offered extra-credit for those who didn't do well on the midterm. He also makes cake for us.

This class was very boring, but George does a great job of making it bearable. I personally did not find the readings necessary, and without doing them I felt I was still able to do quite well. George is also very quick to answer emails and always available for extra help. Overall this class was not as hard as I thought it would be.

Really helpful, engaged in the topic, and tries to make the class as simple as possible (without sacrificing work load unfortunately). Very knowledgeable in the subject but demands a lot of work from students - wouldn't take it with a full or heavy course load. Also recommend taking it with friends so you can create a reading group.

BEST PROF EVER. I love George! I came into theory terrified beyond all reason. Yes it is the most reading you will ever do but he knows how to TEACH! He doesn't simply lecture, he knows how to make topics interesting and clear. He is funny and gives you great class breaks with videos. I can't believe it but...I would love to take the class again!

George is by far the best prof I have ever had at SFU. Although his classes are hard, he always wants his students to succeed and will help you as much as he can to do so. He manages to keep classes interesting (even through the material may be boring) and is always available for extra help.

George is one of the best professors I've ever had at SFU. Archaeological Theory is the toughest class I've ever taken and George does not make it easy. However, his examples such as videos, lectures, and readings are extremely useful when trying to sort theory in your head. Arch 471 - tough course and there's no room to slack off!
Rough class, lots of readings but George is a super nice dude. I am glad everyone else seems to find him strangely hot, because I do too. PS, I'm a guy.

Assigns a LOT of readings, and it's essential that you attend classes because his lecture slides are only pictures, and you need to take your own notes. The prof himself is really great, he's a really good teacher and knows his material. Cares about how you do in the class.

Tons of reading and work for this class-not worth it at all. Theres a 15 pg term paper, and a take home final with 4 essay questions, which you were expected to write 4-6pgs each on! Way too much material, doesn't take into consideration students not familiar with concepts. Very helpful, but brutal. Wants ppl to do well, but doesnt really succeed
Good Guy, works hard, but loads of work

all in all i'm very fond of this proff. But he needs to listen to his students and take a critical eye to his theory course. It's really unreasonable.  BUT, he's a great lecturer, really helpful, if it weren't for the belief in student overload i'd give him the highest rating. And yes, i also occasionally find him strangely hot. Good guy too, cares
Insane amounts of reading, made the class have a VERY large workload, and George seemed to think it was "trimmed down from previous semesters". Nice guy, very helpful, strangely hot.

Listen to what he says not what he writes, that's teh key to getting A's on his exams. He's way to fair giving tones of bonus marks, he wants everyone to succeed. Go see him in office hours if you need help, he's ALWAYS there, overall a great guy!

Great guy. Course blows and he thinks the work load is too small. The couse itself is useless largely irrelevant to any discipline and a waste of paper. Arch theory is not original at all and borrows far too much from real disciplines. Cancel Theory or refund students for taking the course.
George is probably the best professor I've ever had; a good friend, and an incredible academic.  I consider myself lucky to have been taught by him.
Very intense course, would recommend cutting down the readings - as someone who's taken several theory courses you actually learn more this way. George is great though.
Hard Class. Awesome prof!

he's a good proff, he's a very nice man always willing to help a student. But i do take issue with the way arch 471 is set up.  The problem is that every article/book, etc has to be meticulously read/noted and there is just too much to retain in a short semester. The course need better supplements, not a true test of knowledge
professor is fine but course is extremely difficult.
really hard class, need to read alot

Very unorganized, didn't have proper notes to do his lectures from. Went off on random tangents all the time and the textbook was also horrible. He is very vague and you never know what he is looking for in his marking. Very arty-farty. He disses real scientists who actually validate his job of interpreting archeological remains.
George is a great teacher and is very knowledgeable about the subject matter. Feel bad he had to deal with a tonne of jerks in his class last semester. Would definately take a class from him again.

Awesome guy. Extremely helpful and genuinely interested in his students and the class. This class was intro and obviously the subject matter was a bit dry but he did his best to spice it up.  Some real jerks for people took this class which made it awkward, especially when dealing with touchy subjects.  Nicholas did his best to prevent explosions.

Awesome class and awesome prof!!  Nicholas is funny and his material was appropriate for an intro class.  Naysayers were most likely non-arch majors who were looking for an easy credit but didn't get it (class was full of such).  Text was a bit tedious and readings for quizzes were heavy, but George was great.  I will take anything I can with him.
Unless you want to die of boredom.. do not take this class!  You will learn NOTHING!!!

Despite the material, Nichols tried very hard to present the topic as clearly as possible. He put himself out there for the students to take advantage of. A really nice guy, you could tell that he cared about the course. It did not help that there were rude people in the course as well as sneaky people who were filled with hate and spite. Great Guy

Terrible. Very hard to get a grip on anything concrete or understandable in this class. If you ask a question, he rambles on about somthing different istead of saving everyones time and just saying 'i don't know'.  This guy disses real scientists and thinks hes funny.  Trying to study was hard because the notes from his lectures are so bad.
